text:
Multitask prompted finetuning (MTF) has proven highly effective at enabling large language models to generalize to unseen tasks in zero-shot settings. However, research in this area has primarily focused on English-only models and datasets. In this work, we apply MTF to the multilingual BLOOM and mT5 model families, resulting in two new sets of finetuned models: BLOOMZ and mT0.

Our experiments demonstrate that finetuning multilingual models on English tasks with English prompts significantly enhances their ability to generalize to non-English tasks, even when those languages were only encountered during pretraining. By extending the training mixture to include multilingual tasks and machine-translated prompts—a collection we introduce as xP3—we achieve further performance gains and establish new state-of-the-art results in zero-shot crosslingual benchmarks. 

Remarkably, we observe that these models can generalize to tasks in languages that were not intentionally included in either pretraining or finetuning, suggesting the acquisition of higher-level, language-agnostic capabilities. We conclude that multitask finetuning on a diverse range of languages and tasks is a robust strategy for developing capable multilingual assistants. Our code, datasets, and models are made publicly available.
